title: Colombia Plane Crash Kills 66: Search Underway For Four Missing Soldiers
description: Sixty-six Colombian security personnel are dead and four missing after a Hercules military transport plane crashed near the Peruvian border.
lede: A Colombian military transport plane crashed shortly after takeoff on Monday near the Peruvian border, killing 66 security personnel. Rescue workers and locals are desperately searching for four soldiers who remain missing. The disaster has ignited fierce criticism from President Gustavo Petro regarding the state of the nation's military hardware.

sections.heading: What Happened
paragraphs: The Hercules C-130 transport plane went down minutes after departing from Puerto Leguízamo, en route to Puerto Asís. A total of 128 people were on board the aircraft when it plummeted into trees near a residential area, according to eyewitnesses.

Locals were the first responders at the fiery crash site, rescuing dozens of injured survivors from the wreckage and transporting them to nearby hospitals on motorcycles. Mobile phone footage revealed the plane losing altitude rapidly before a massive plume of smoke and explosions engulfed the area.

Commander General Hugo Alejandro López confirmed the devastating toll: 58 army personnel, six air force members, and two police officers were killed. Defense Ministry officials noted the sounds of explosions were caused by ammunition detonating in the flames, ruling out an attack by armed groups active in the Putumayo region.

sections.heading: Why It Matters
paragraphs: The catastrophic loss of life represents a severe blow to the Colombian armed forces and highlights systemic issues with their aviation fleet. The tragedy immediately prompted President Gustavo Petro to publicly condemn the military's equipment, describing the crashed aircraft as "scrap metal" purchased in 2020.

Petro utilized social media to criticize bureaucratic hurdles that have stalled his plans to modernize the armed forces. He emphasized that no further delays would be tolerated, citing the immense human cost of operating outdated and dangerous machinery.

sections.heading: The Bigger Picture
paragraphs: This incident is part of a disturbing pattern of aviation failures involving the Hercules C-130 model in South America. Just weeks prior, on February 27, another Hercules C-130 operated by the Bolivian army overshot a runway in El Alto and crashed into a highway, resulting in 24 fatalities.

The repeated accidents raise serious regional concerns about the maintenance, age, and safety protocols surrounding these widely used military transport planes. For Colombia, operating in challenging jungle terrain against armed insurgencies demands reliable air mobility, making these fleet vulnerabilities a critical national security risk.

sections.heading: What's Next
paragraphs: An official investigation is currently underway to determine the exact mechanical or operational failures that led to the crash. Investigators will need to sift through the heavily burned wreckage and analyze the circumstances of the aircraft's recent procurement and maintenance history.

Meanwhile, search and rescue operations remain laser-focused on locating the four missing soldiers in the dense southern border region. The Colombian government is expected to face intense pressure to accelerate military modernization efforts and prevent future tragedies.
